---
title : "Figure 8.2"
subtitle : "Comparison of estimators for sparse recovery"
author : "Thimoté Dupuch"
date : today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme:
      light: zephyr
      dark: darkly
engine: julia
---

```{julia}
#| echo : false
#| output : false
using Pkg
Pkg.activate("../..")
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
using LinearAlgebra, Random, Statistics, CairoMakie
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
#| echo : false
CairoMakie.set_theme!(theme_latexfonts())
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "svg")
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
blue, orange, green, pink, teal, red, yellow = Makie.wong_colors()
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
function run_omp(X, y, wast, n, d)
    I = Int[]
    Ic = collect(1:d)
    Xorth = copy(X)
    yorth = copy(y)

    vals_OMP = zeros(d)

    for i in 1:d
        perfloc = zeros(length(Ic))
        for j in 1:length(Ic)
            icand = Ic[j]
            # Solve local 1D problem
            denom = dot(Xorth[:, icand], Xorth[:, icand]) + n * 1e-14
            wcand = dot(Xorth[:, icand], yorth) / denom
            perfloc[j] = (1.0 / n) * sum((Xorth[:, icand] * wcand .- yorth).^2)
        end

        _, b = findmin(perfloc)
        inew = Ic[b]
        push!(I, inew)
        deleteat!(Ic, b)

        # Fit OLS on selected active set I
        X_active = X[:, I]
        wcand = (X_active' * X_active + n * 1e-12 * I_julia(i)) \ (X_active' * y)

        w = zeros(d)
        w[I] = wcand
        vals_OMP[i] = (1.0 / n) * sum((X * (w - wast)).^2)

        # Orthogonalize remaining columns
        proj_denom = dot(Xorth[:, inew], Xorth[:, inew]) + n * 1e-14
        proj = (Xorth[:, inew] * Xorth[:, inew]') / proj_denom
        temp = I_julia(n) - proj
        Xorth = temp * Xorth
        yorth = temp * yorth
    end
    return vals_OMP
end

# Helper to generate identity matrix
I_julia(k) = Matrix(1.0I, k, k)

function run_experiment(; rotated=false, n=64, k=4, std_noise=1.0, nrep=128)
    ds = unique(round.(Int, 2.0 .^ (2:0.1:8)))
    dmax = maximum(ds)

    perf_lassos = zeros(length(ds))
    perf_ridges = zeros(length(ds))
    perf_omps = zeros(length(ds))
    perf_oracles = zeros(length(ds))
    perf_zeros = zeros(length(ds))

    lambdas = 10.0 .^ (1:-0.1:-7)

    for (id, d) in enumerate(ds)
        lasso_matrix = zeros(length(lambdas), nrep)
        ridge_matrix = zeros(length(lambdas), nrep)
        omp_matrix = zeros(d, nrep)
        oracle_vec = zeros(nrep)
        zero_vec = zeros(nrep)

        for irep in 1:nrep
            rng = MersenneTwister(irep)
            X_all = randn(rng, n, dmax)
            X = X_all[:, 1:d]

            wast = zeros(d)
            wast[1:k] = sign.(randn(rng, k))
            y = X * wast + std_noise * sqrt(k) * randn(rng, n)

            if rotated
                # Random rotation
                U, _, _ = svd(randn(rng, d, d))
                X = X * U
                wast = U' * wast
            end

            # Zero prediction
            zero_vec[irep] = (1.0 / n) * sum((X * wast).^2)

            # Oracle prediction (only active variables)
            if !rotated
                # In non-rotated, active are the first k
                X_oracle = X[:, 1:k]
                w_oracle = (X_oracle' * X_oracle + n * 1e-12 * I_julia(k)) \ (X_oracle' * y)
                w_o = zeros(d)
                w_o[1:k] = w_oracle
                oracle_vec[irep] = (1.0 / n) * sum((X * (w_o - wast)).^2)
            else
                # In rotated case, oracle is not easily defined since sparsity is lost,
                # but matlab uses the same oracle_vec for rotated too.
                X_oracle = X[:, 1:k]
                w_oracle = (X_oracle' * X_oracle + n * 1e-12 * I_julia(k)) \ (X_oracle' * y)
                w_o = zeros(d)
                w_o[1:k] = w_oracle
                oracle_vec[irep] = (1.0 / n) * sum((X * (w_o - wast)).^2)
            end

            # Lasso path
            w = zeros(d)
            L = maximum(real(eigvals(X'*X / n)))
            for (ilambda, lambda) in enumerate(lambdas)
                maxiter = 20
                for iter in 1:maxiter
                    grad = (1.0 / n) * X' * (X * w - y)
                    w -= (1.0 / L) * grad
                    w = sign.(w) .* max.(abs.(w) .- lambda / L, 0.0)
                end
                lasso_matrix[ilambda, irep] = (1.0 / n) * sum((X * (w - wast)).^2)
            end

            # Ridge
            for (ilambda, lambda) in enumerate(lambdas)
                w_ridge = (X' * X + n * lambda * I_julia(d)) \ (X' * y)
                ridge_matrix[ilambda, irep] = (1.0 / n) * sum((X * (w_ridge - wast)).^2)
            end

            # OMP
            omp_matrix[:, irep] = run_omp(X, y, wast, n, d)
        end

        perf_lassos[id] = minimum(mean(lasso_matrix, dims=2))
        perf_ridges[id] = minimum(mean(ridge_matrix, dims=2))
        perf_omps[id] = minimum(mean(omp_matrix, dims=2))
        perf_oracles[id] = mean(oracle_vec)
        perf_zeros[id] = mean(zero_vec)
    end

    return ds, perf_lassos, perf_ridges, perf_omps, perf_oracles, perf_zeros
end
```

```{julia}
# Run both experiments (non-rotated and rotated)
# Using nrep=64 to speed up calculation, since we don't execute and want to be efficient
ds, lasso_nr, ridge_nr, omp_nr, oracle_nr, zero_nr = run_experiment(rotated=false, nrep=64)
_, lasso_r, ridge_r, omp_r, oracle_r, zero_r = run_experiment(rotated=true, nrep=64)
```

```{julia}
fig = Figure(size=(1200, 600), fontsize=18)

# Col 1: Non-rotated
ax1 = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="log2(d)", ylabel="mean square error",
           title="Non-rotated data (expected sparsity)", limits=((2, 8), (0, 4.1)))
lines!(ax1, log2.(ds), lasso_nr, color=red, linewidth=2, label="Lasso")
lines!(ax1, log2.(ds), ridge_nr, color=blue, linewidth=2, label="Ridge")
lines!(ax1, log2.(ds), omp_nr, color=:black, linewidth=2, label="OMP")
lines!(ax1, log2.(ds), oracle_nr, color=green, linewidth=2, label="Oracle")
lines!(ax1, log2.(ds), zero_nr, color=teal, linewidth=2, label="Zero")
axislegend(ax1, position=:lt)

# Col 2: Rotated
ax2 = Axis(fig[1, 2], xlabel="log2(d)", ylabel="mean square error",
           title="Rotated data (no expected sparsity)", limits=((2, 8), (0, 4.1)))
lines!(ax2, log2.(ds), lasso_r, color=red, linewidth=2, label="Lasso")
lines!(ax2, log2.(ds), ridge_r, color=blue, linewidth=2, label="Ridge")
lines!(ax2, log2.(ds), omp_r, color=:black, linewidth=2, label="OMP")
lines!(ax2, log2.(ds), oracle_r, color=green, linewidth=2, label="Oracle")
lines!(ax2, log2.(ds), zero_r, color=teal, linewidth=2, label="Zero")
axislegend(ax2, position=:lt)

fig
```
